ENGNew: Barley Tea, Black Tea, and a Stone Mill
changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Barley Tea and Black Tea and a Millstone"

TextENGNew: "Your blood pressure has improved quite nicely. To recover this well at your age... I must say, I'm impressed."
"At this rate, you could easily live to a hundred or two hundred, Oryou-san."
changeReason: Old exaggerates with 'a couple hundred more years' while new literally translates 百でも二百でも as 'a hundred or two hundred' (age), changing the scale of the compliment.

TextENGNew: The young doctor smiled as he said this, peeling the blood pressure cuff from the arm of the old woman tucked into her futon.
"You do flatter me, Dr. Irie... A stubborn old thing like me should hurry up and die before I get in the young folk's way...@k@vS20/17/442300002....Ho ho ho ho..."
The old woman -- Oryou -- flashed a sly grin and let out a thin, raspy laugh.
changeReason: Old describes a 'broad smile' and 'faint laugh' while new captures ニヤリと as 'sly grin' and adds 'raspy', better conveying the cunning quality of Oryou's expression.

TextENGNew: Then, turning towards the sliding door, she called out in a strong voice.
"Is Shimiko-san or Taeko-san about? Get Dr. Irie some barley tea!"
changeReason: Old translates 麦茶 as generic 'tea' while new correctly specifies 'barley tea', which is significant in context since the tip's title references barley tea and black tea as distinct drinks.

TextENGNew: The sound of rushed footsteps from the hallway drew closer, until finally the door slid smoothly open.
There stood a young girl.@k...She appeared to be the old woman's granddaughter.
``Shimiko-san already left for the day. ...Do you need something?``
``Mion, make some barley tea for Dr. Irie.``
``Okay, got it. Did you want some too, Granny?@k@vS20/26/440300107. Or would black tea be better?@k@vS20/26/440300108. Want lots of milk and sugar?``
``I'll measure it out myself, so you don't have to put any in. Just bring the sugar jar and some milk when you bring the tea.``
``Kay kay.``
The girl called Mion tossed a casual reply at her demanding grandmother and disappeared back down the hallway.
``Pour the doctor's tea in the cups for the guests!! And make sure you bring a coaster too!@k@vS20/17/442300007. Also make sure to dry the outside of the glass, okay!?``
``Yeah, yeah. You're such a fussypants.``
An unfazed voice drifted back from down the corridor.
changeReason: Old translates へこたれない声 as 'tired voice' which is the opposite of the Japanese meaning ('unfazed/undaunted voice'); new correctly uses 'unfazed voice'.

TextENGNew: The lack of earnestness was nothing new.@kThe old woman muttered something about the girl being hopeless and smiled wryly.
changeReason: Old says the old lady 'chided the girl' while new says she 'muttered something about the girl being hopeless and smiled wryly', which matches the Japanese 仕方ないヤツと漏らし苦笑; the old inaccurately implies active scolding.

TextENGNew: ``Geez, that girl just won't learn. Nothing I ever scold her about sticks.``
``Now now, Oryou-san... you don't have to say that. Mion-chan is doing her best in her own way, even though she's so young.``
``Her mother was the same way... couldn't teach her anything. The apple doesn't fall far from the tree!``
``Aha ha ha ha. So her mother's mother must be exactly the same, no?``
``Kha ha ha ha......!``
The old woman snorted, then burst into a hearty, full-throated laugh.@kShe didn't seem to mind the joke at all.
``Dr. Irie. I'm sorry, but could you open the doors for me?@k@vS20/17/442300011. It seems there's a nice cool breeze outside.``
Before they knew it, the cool song of the evening cicadas had begun seeping through the gaps in the sliding door.
changeReason: Old preserves the series-specific term 'higurashi' while new replaces it with the generic 'evening cicadas'; for a series titled 'Higurashi', maintaining the Japanese term carries thematic weight.

TextENGNew: Irie stood up, and opened the door a bit.
...A refreshing breeze swept through, chasing the stale air from the room.
``It's gotten fairly hot during the day, but the evenings are still rather cool, aren't they. It was almost downright chilly last night.``
``Yeah. ...Those types of mornings and evenings are another one of Hinamizawa's fine points.``
Irie replied with a smile as he returned to sit on the cushion beside the old lady.
And the two of them, for a little while, immersed themselves in the cries of the higurashi...
"I intend to live to a hundred, you know. ...Until the dam business is well and truly settled, the lid on my coffin won't stay shut."
``...To get the government to withdraw a decision they've already made is a fairly difficult thing to do...``
"The way the government works is like turning a millstone. And a fearfully heavy one at that."
``...A millstone?``
``Don'tcha know?? A millstone.``
Irie quickly affirmed that of course he knew what the old lady was talking about.
For he knew full well that Oryou didn't like having her stories interrupted.
"This millstone of the government's, see... it grinds everything to dust. Quite a thing."
"But it don't turn easy, because it's a terrible heavy millstone.@k@vS20/17/442300018.Takes a great many folk all heavin' together just to get it creaking along. That's the kind of millstone it is."
Irie pursed his lips, listening quietly to what she was saying.
Eventually Mion returned with some tea laid out on a tray.
Seeing that Oryou was talking and in a good mood, she quietly knelt down and laid out the sets for the barley and black tea, careful not to interrupt.

"That's why once it gets going, it ain't easily stopped. ...Getting it moving in the first place takes the most effort.@k@vS20/17/442300020.Nobody wants to go through that again, so everyone keeps pushing without rest."
``You're talking about friction, right? I get what you're saying.``
"In other words. If by some mishap the millstone suddenly stops... getting it turning again takes a tremendous amount of force."
``...Certainly, to get a project that's been suspended going again takes quite a lot of work.``
"It ain't a millstone that stops easy. ...But once it does stop, it'll never turn again.@k@vS20/17/442300023.That's the kind of millstone it is."
``It'd be nice if there was some way to get that millstone to stop, wouldn't it?``
When Irie responded with that, Mion and the old lady suddenly sunk into silence...
Irie, instinctively thinking he had said something rude, searched in a small panic for the words to correct himself.
But it was not a silence born of offense.
...For what had crept across both Oryou's and Mion's faces were cold, knowing smiles.
The atmosphere surrounding Irie suddenly froze, leaving him flabbergasted.
...Thinking that the scornful smiles creeping across both of their faces was the result of some mistake of his, he could only grow afraid.
``...Haha, hahahahahahaha...``
It hadn't been that long at all since he was enveloped by silence.
...But Irie could not endure the silence, and could only laugh vaguely to cover his unease.
...Irie's awkward laughter eventually spread to the old woman and Mion, and the room filled with a laughter whose cause nobody could quite name -- a laughter that left a faint chill on the skin.
...Only the higurashi refused to join in, continuing their simple, unchanging chorus...
